author: sproul
content:
I'm late to the party but here are some thoughts on the non-canonical PTC thing: https://github.com/ethereum/beacon-APIs/pull/619#issuecomment-4786553194

I think I share <@425572898787426305>'s scepticism that this sort of feature is useful. More detail at that link

author: potuz
content: I think this feature is only impactful when there is no minority chain: the split is mostly even and we are syncing blocks from both sides of the chain

author: m.kalinin
content: even then there is no incentive for builders to reveal payloads and protocol doesn't enforce the payment. from my perspective with self-building ptc votes play much less important role in the network where beacon block reorgs become more viable

author: potuz
content: I don't see why that's the case though. P2P builders will most likely be automated setups like my node that won't be sophisticated enough. Also, reorging the payload is rational regardless if it was self built or not

author: m.kalinin
content: builder will be looking at the attestation rate supporting a beacon block before they reveal a payload, rationale builder won't reveal payload on any of the two competing forks as there will probably be less support than it is required, although maybe one of the forks will meet the required threshold, it is 40%, right?

author: potuz
content: It's 60, no threshold will be reached in the split scenario.  so builder is never forced to pay in that case

author: m.kalinin
content: rationale builders will have automation to handle low participation cases and they will likely not reveal payloads. what happens in this case? fallback to self building?

author: potuz
content: if you do not get any bid from p2p or direct connection you are left with only your local block

author: m.kalinin
content: but if you propose with a bid but no reveal happens for a number of slots?

author: potuz
content: we will have different types of circuit breakers, they aren't yet implemented as far as I know

author: m.kalinin
content: In the situation where there are two competing branches, a network will very likely fallback to self building because builders being rationale won't be revealing payloads according to their scripts. In this case by PTC voting on all branches a network will protect self building from a dishonest proposer that attempts to reorg self-built payload. This is definitely plausible, but dishonest proposer in this scenario is quite close to reorg a beacon block with proposer boost
